---
title: "Career Plan"
---

# Jacky's Career Plan

## Apply Technical Skills More Directly to My Work

I already have a solid foundation in tools like Python, SQL, and data analysis, but the biggest opportunity is applying them more consistently to real business problems for clients.

Over the next few months, I plan to:

- Spend more time working with internal datasets to analyze pricing strategy in the marketplace and how it impacts leads per unit
- Rebuild or improve existing reports and dashboards to better understand how pricing, inventory, and performance metrics connect
- Start incorporating more advanced techniques like basic modeling or segmentation to identify patterns in pricing and lead performance

## Improve How I Translate Analysis Into Insights

A key gap for me is turning technical work into clear, actionable insights.

To improve this, I will focus on:

- Communicating findings in a simple and clear way
- Connecting analysis back to business outcomes instead of just reporting numbers
- Bringing more data-driven insights into conversations and decision-making

## Build Experience Through Projects and Collaboration

To keep developing, I need to consistently apply what I’m learning and make that work visible.

I plan to:

- Continue building hands-on projects using real datasets, including ones similar to the work I do at CarGurus
- Share these projects internally so other teams can use them and benefit from the insights
- Keep collaborating with my teammate to combine technical and business strengths

My focus is on strengthening technical skills, improving how I communicate insights, and continuing to build real, applied experience through projects and collaboration, with the goal of getting more involved in data-focused work within CarGurus over time.
